basics.name: Zijin Chen
basics.summary: PhD researcher specialising in Southern Ocean and Antarctic sea-ice processes, with a strong focus on sea-ice-driven water-mass transformation (WMT), freshwater redistribution, and ocean ventilation and carbon uptake in CMIP6 climate models. Extensive experience in large-scale model diagnostics, inter-model comparison, and Python-based analysis on HPC systems. Actively preparing multiple journal manuscripts and seeking post-PhD research opportunities in polar climate and ocean-ice interactions.
basics.label: PhD Candidate | Physical Oceanography & Climate Science
basics.affiliation: Australian Antarctic Partnership Program (AAPP) & University of Tasmania

publications.name: The impact of Antarctic sea ice on Southern Ocean water mass transformation in coupled climate models
publications.summary: Chen, Z., Hobbs, W., Chase, Z., & Zika, J. Published article on how Antarctic sea ice shapes Southern Ocean water-mass transformation in coupled climate models.

publications.name: Drivers of inter-model spread in sea-ice-driven water-mass transformation
publications.summary: PhD Chapter 2 manuscript led by Zijin Chen examining the sources of inter-model spread in sea-ice-driven water-mass transformation diagnostics.

publications.name: Role of Southern Ocean ventilation in modulating carbon uptake
publications.summary: PhD Chapter 3 manuscript on the links between Southern Ocean ventilation changes and carbon uptake variability.
